channel.title: Tech
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from Tech, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Score, the dating app for people with good credit, is back
item.description: Two years ago, a controversial dating app was launched and quickly shuttered: for people with good-to-excellent credit. Now, the founder is relaunchin...

item.title: Cohere’s $240M year sets stage for IPO
item.description: Cohere surpassed $240 million in annual recurring revenue in 2025, highlighting strong enterprise AI demand as the Canadian startup positions itself f...

item.title: Dutch phone giant Odido says millions of customers affected by data breach
item.description: The Dutch phone giant Odido is the latest phone and internet company to be hacked in recent months, as governments and financially motivated hackers c...

item.title: Why top talent is walking away from OpenAI and xAI
item.description: AI companies have been hemorrhaging talent the past few weeks. Half of xAI’s founding team has left the company — some on their own, others through “r...

item.title: The SEC closed its investigation into Fisker
item.description: The financial regulator revealed in a response to a TechCrunch records request that the probe was closed in September 2025.

item.title: Amazon’s Ring cancels partnership with Flock, a network of AI cameras used by ICE, feds, and police
item.description: This news comes less than a week after Ring's Super Bowl commercial stoked controversy over the company's capacity for mass surveillance.

item.title: OpenAI removes access to sycophancy-prone GPT-4o model
item.description: The model is known for its overly sycophantic nature and its role in several lawsuits involving users' unhealthy relationships to the chatbot.

item.title: Sex toys maker Tenga says hacker stole customer information
item.description: The Japanese sex toy maker said a hacker broke into an employee's inbox and stole customer names, email addresses, and correspondence, including order...

item.title: Fintech lending giant Figure confirms data breach
item.description: The company said hackers downloaded “a limited number of files” after breaking into an employee’s account. The hacking group ShinyHunters took respons...

item.title: Meta plans to add facial recognition to its smart glasses, report claims
item.description: The feature, internally known as “Name Tag,” would allow smart glasses wearers to identify people and get information about them via Meta's AI assista...
